title: Turbomachine blade with protective structure, turbomachine, and method of forming a protective structure
abstract: A turbomachine blade (1) is provided with an airfoil portion (2) having a leading edge (3) and a trailing edge (4) and first and second sides (5, 6) connecting the leading edge (3) and the trailing edge (4), and with a protective structure comprising a protective edging (7) and first and second protective stripes (8, 9); the protective edging (7) is located on the leading edge (3); the first protective stripe (8) is located on the first protective side (5) and adjacent to the edging (7); the second protective stripe (9) is located on the second protective side (6) and adjacent to the edging (7); the edging (7) is formed by laser cladding or welding or plasma spraying or detonation spraying or wire arc spraying or flame spraying or high velocity oxyfuel coating spraying or warm spraying; the first and second stripes (8, 9) are formed by cold spraying.
claims: Claims CLAIMS : 1. A turbomachine blade (1) with an airfoil portion (2) having a leading edge (3) and a trailing edge (4) and first and second sides (5, 6) connecting the leading edge (3) and the trailing edge (4), and with a protective structure comprising a protective edging (7) and first and second protective stripes (8, 9); wherein the protective edging (7) is located on the leading edge (3); wherein the first protective stripe (8) is located on the first side (5) and adjacent to the edging (7); wherein the second protective stripe (9) is located on the second side (6) and adjacent to the edging (7); wherein the edging (7) is formed by laser cladding or welding or plasma spraying or detonation spraying or wire arc spraying or flame spraying or high velocity oxyfuel coating spraying or warm spraying; wherein the first and second stripes (8, 9) are formed by cold spraying. 2. The turbomachine blade (1) of claim 1 , wherein the airfoil portion (2) is made of iron, titanium, nickel base alloy or stainless steel. 3. The turbomachine blade (1) of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the edging (7) is made of cobalt base alloy or cobalt-chromium alloy. 4. The turbomachine blade (1) of any preceding claim, wherein the first and second stripes (8, 9) are made of cobalt base alloy or cobalt-chromium alloy. 5. The turbomachine blade (1) of any preceding claim, wherein the edging (7) and the first and second stripes (8, 9) extend over from 20% to 50% of the height of the airfoil portion (2). 6. The turbomachine blade (1) of any preceding claim, wherein the first and second stripes (8, 9) extend over from 5% to 50%> of the width of the airfoil portion (2). 7. A turbomachine, in particular a steam turbine, comprising a plurality of turbomachine blades according to any of the preceding claims. 8. A method of forming a protective structure on a turbomachine blade (1), wherein, the method comprises the following successive steps: A) providing a turbomachine blade (1) comprising an airfoil portion (2) having a leading edge (3) and a trailing edge (4) and first and second sides (5, 6) connecting the leading edge (3) and the trailing edge (4), B) forming a protective edging (7) on the leading edge (3) by laser cladding, and C) forming first and second protective stripes (8, 9) on the first and second sides (5, 6) adjacently to the edging by cold spraying through a spraying nozzle. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the first protective stripe (8) is formed before or after or contemporaneously with the second protective stripes (9). 10. The method of claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the stripes (8, 9) comprises a plurality of parallel segments (81 , 91) transversal to the leading edge (3). 1 1. The method of any of claims 8 to 10, wherein, in step C, the spraying nozzle moves from a side (5, 6) of the airfoil portion toward the edging (7) and rotates by at least 40Â°, preferably 80-90Â°. 12. The method of any of claims 8 to 1 1 , wherein step C comprises: - a first sub-step C3 wherein the spraying nozzle moves from the first side (5) of the airfoil portion (2) toward the edging (7) thus forming a segment (81) of the first stripe (8), - a second sub-step C4 wherein the spraying nozzle rotates about the leading edge (3) by 80-180Â° thus forming a connecting segment, - a third sub-step C5 wherein the spraying nozzle moves away from the edging (7) to the second side (6) of the airfoil portion (2) thus forming a segment (91) of the second stripe (9). 13. The method of claim 12, wherein step C comprises the following sub-steps after sub-step C5 : - a fourth sub-step C6 wherein the spraying nozzle moves from the second side of the airfoil portion toward the edging thus forming a segment of the second stripe adjacent to the previous one, - a fifth sub-step C7 wherein the spraying nozzle rotates about the leading edge by 80-180Â° thus forming a connecting segment, - a sixth sub-step C8 wherein the spraying nozzle moves away from the edging to the first side of the airfoil portion thus forming a segment of the first stripe adjacent to the previous one; wherein the spraying nozzle is displaced longitudinally after sub-step C5 and before sub-step C6. 14. The method of any of claims 8 to 13, wherein the stripes (108) comprises a plurality of parallel segments (181) parallel to the leading edge (103).
